text.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework Checklists (2025-2026) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the WA checklist. Each rule pairs a binary check + an AWS-source rationale.

rule.statement: The checklist MUST contain at least one item per pillar (OpEx, Security, Reliability, Performance, Cost, Sustainability). No pillar may be empty.
rule.rationale: Skipping a pillar means the workload has not been reviewed against it. The Sustainability pillar (added 2021) is the most-skipped — and the easiest to dismiss.

rule.statement: Every item MUST be phrased as a binary pass/fail check. Items like 'review security posture' are forbidden; 'IAM root account MFA enabled' is allowed.
rule.rationale: Non-binary items defer the decision to the reviewer's gut — defeating the point of a checklist. Binary items make the review reproducible across reviewers.

rule.statement: Every item MUST name an AWS CLI / Config rule / Compute Optimizer call that verifies pass/fail. Items relying on human inspection are forbidden.
rule.rationale: CLI-backed items can be wired into a nightly job for drift detection. Manual items decay between reviews.

rule.statement: Every failed item MUST carry a severity (HIGH / MED / LOW) based on impact + compliance scope. HIGH items block production launch.
rule.rationale: Without severity, remediation degenerates into 'fix everything' (impossible) or 'fix what I noticed' (random). Severity drives priority.

rule.statement: An audit-clean workload MUST be re-reviewed at least quarterly. A new workload MUST have a clean checklist before its first production deployment.
rule.rationale: Cloud environments drift; quarterly cadence catches drift before it becomes a CVE. Pre-prod gate prevents debt from entering production at all.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
